Aotearoa New Zealand’s AI adoption has shifted from experimentation to practical, governed use, strongest in agriculture, healthcare administration, education support, public-sector back-office work, finance decision support, and increasingly structured academic research and creative production. Uptake remains uneven in high-stakes or frontline decisions, with progress constrained by governance, human oversight, privacy and cyber requirements, infrastructure and capability gaps, and concerns around trust, cultural rights, and data sovereignty.

Updated 11 March 2026
